channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cardoso criticises PSL fixture schedule and refereeing after Sundowns fall behind Pirates
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns coach Miguel Cardoso has expressed strong criticism towards the Premier Soccer League (PSL) and match officials, claiming that both have favoured Orlando Pirates during this season. ...

item.title: PTI Claims Imran Khan Suffers Eye Damage and Health Risks During Jail Term
item.description: ISLAMABAD: PTI Secretary General Salman Akram Raja on Wednesday alleged that Imran Khan, the founder of Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI), has lost vision in one eye due to torture during his imprisonmen...

item.title: Gowon releases autobiography explaining his leadership and key decisions
item.description: Former Head of State, General Yakubu Gowon (rtd), don release him autobiography wey dey explain the tough decisions wey him take when him dey lead Nigeria and give better insight into one of di most c...

item.title: Scientists develop method to detect sound from black hole mergers
item.description: Scientists have developed a groundbreaking technique that allows them to detect and 'hear' collisions between black holes. These cosmic events, which occur billions of light-years away, produce gravit...

item.title: 'Almost Us' grosses P23 million in its first week, remains in theaters for second week
item.description: The Filipino romantic drama 'Almost Us' has achieved a notable milestone at the local box office, earning a total of P23 million during its opening week. The film's strong performance has encouraged i...

item.title: Three women found deceased in the sea near Brighton
item.description: Authorities have confirmed that the bodies of three women have been recovered from the sea near Brighton. Emergency services were called to the scene following reports of people in distress in the wat...

item.title: Albanese Government Responds to Peta Murphy’s Gambling Reform Report After Nearly Three Years
item.description: The Albanese government has finally responded to the 2023 'You Win Some, You Lose More' report into online gambling harms, chaired by the late Labor MP Peta Murphy. The report, commissioned in Septemb...

item.title: EAC Court Suspends Tanzania's Tax on Kenyan Safety Matches
item.description: The East African Court of Justice has given a temporary order stopping Tanzania from collecting a new excise duty on safety matches made in Kenya. The tax, set at Tsh400 per kilogram under Tanzania's ...

item.title: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and Centre Cooperating Closely to Combat Terrorism
item.description: Defence Minister Khawaja Asif, speaking in the National Assembly on Wednesday, confirmed that Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP) government is fully cooperating with the federal government in tackling terrorism....

item.title: Moderna and Korea University Collaborate on Early-Stage Hantavirus mRNA Vaccine
item.description: Since 2023, Moderna and Korea University’s Vaccine Innovation Center (VIC-K) have been collaborating to develop an mRNA-based vaccine targeting hantavirus. This comes amid global awareness of hantavir...

item.title: Chéri Hérouard and His Iconic Contributions to La Vie Parisienne
item.description: Chéri Hérouard (1881–1961) was a celebrated French illustrator whose work defined the Golden Age of French magazine art. Best known for his long-standing contributions to La Vie Parisienne, Hérouard’s...

item.title: Simi Sasagi to miss four-to-six weeks with shoulder injury, Raiders manage limited second-row options
item.description: Canberra Raiders forward Simi Sasagi has received encouraging news regarding his left shoulder injury, which is expected to sideline him for four-to-six weeks. Scans confirmed there is no structural d...

item.title: How a Vancouver woman preserved and shared her mother’s cherished necklace
item.description: Cara, a 44-year-old communications specialist in Vancouver, recently inherited a meaningful piece of jewellery from her late mother: a 20-inch gold necklace with a pendant engraved with a Spanish phra...

item.title: Karachi Police Arrest Pinky Over Multi-Year Drug Supply Network
item.description: Karachi Police have arrested Anmol, also known as Pinky, for running a drug supply network across the city. The arrest took place in the Garden area, and she is now in custody on a three-day physical ...

item.title: Canadian Soldiers Rescue Abandoned Baby in England During WWII
item.description: On September 23, 1941, a newborn baby was abandoned in Horsell Common, a park near London. Three Canadian soldiers—Gunner Robert C. Griffin from Regina, Saskatchewan, Lance Sergeant Ernest Curtis from...

item.title: Researchers Identify First AI-Generated Zero-Day Exploit Amid Rising Cybersecurity Risks
item.description: At the end of 2025, researchers found that widely available AI tools, often used for productivity, can also be misused for cyberattacks. These tools make it easier for attackers to write sophisticated...

item.title: India raises import duties on gold and silver amid rupee decline
item.description: India has decided to increase import duties on gold and silver significantly, aiming to protect its weakening rupee and support foreign currency reserves. The government more than doubled taxes from 6...

item.title: Labour Party in Nasarawa Elects Asoloko Maku as State Chairman Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Labour Party for Nasarawa State don elect new chairman, Asoloko Maku, wey go lead the party as dem dey prepare for 2027 elections. For press briefing wey happen for Lafia, Maku talk say the recent con...

item.title: The Life and Legacy of Soraya Esfandiary-Bakhtiary: Queen of Iran and European Socialite
item.description: Soraya Esfandiary-Bakhtiary (1932–2001) was the second wife of Shah Mohammad Reza Pahlavi, serving as Queen of Iran from 1951 until their divorce in 1958. Her marriage, though initially celebrated, fa...

item.title: BNXN and Sarz drop five-track EP 'The Game Needs Us', climbs Apple Music Nigeria charts
item.description: Naija music fans don get reason to dey ginger as BNXN and Sarz don release their joint EP, 'The Game Needs Us'. The EP get five tracks and e land for Apple Music Nigeria Top Albums chart at number two...
